summary: Possible prettier typosquat with base64 decode in lib/mirror.js
details: Package name 'prettier_v1' closely resembles the widely-used 'prettier' formatter, which raises typosquat-confusion concerns. lib/mirror.js contains a Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8') decode pattern, which is commonly used to obscure embedded payloads but is also used in many benign contexts. Reachability of this decode (whether it executes at install/import or only on explicit invocation) and the nature of the decoded content were not fully traced. Combined name-similarity plus base64 decoding warrants human review before allowing into a build.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
